sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking white block
seg = current_frame.segmentation
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation

print("Current game area objects:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 9:
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

print("\nPrevious game area objects:")
for node in prev_seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 9:
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Also check top bar objects
print("\nTop bar objects (current):")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    if min(rows) < 9 and node['pixels'] < 100:
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Again only the timer bar changed (id=15 pixels decreased from 61 to 60). The game objects didn't change. Clicking on the white block didn't do anything meaningful.
Let me reconsider the game. Looking at the top bar more carefully:
- There's a white 3x3 block (id=6) at [3,36]-[5,38] inside a charcoal border (id=3)
- There's a purple 3x3 block (id=7) at [3,42]-[5,44] inside a charcoal border (id=4)
- There's also a white strip (id=8) at [7,41]-[7,45] below the purple block
These look like selectable items in a toolbar. Maybe I need to click on one of these to select a color/tool, then do something else.
Also, the top-left panel shows white on top and purple on bottom - this could be the goal state pattern.
Let me try clicking on the white block in the top bar (the small one at [3,36]-[5,38]).
